L. 102–354section 585 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: submit only certain issues in controversy to arbitration; or

children.label: B
children.text: arbitration on the condition that the award must be within a range of possible outcomes.

The arbitration agreement that sets forth the subject matter submitted to the arbitrator shall be in writing. Each such arbitration agreement shall specify a maximum award that may be issued by the arbitrator and may specify other conditions limiting the range of possible outcomes.
An agency may not require any person to consent to arbitration as a condition of entering into a contract or obtaining a benefit.
would otherwise have authority to enter into a settlement concerning the matter; or
is otherwise specifically authorized by the agency to consent to the use of arbitration.

children.label: c
children.text: Prior to using binding arbitration under this subchapter, the head of an agency, in consultation with the Attorney General and after taking into account the factors in section 572(b), shall issue guidance on the appropriate use of binding arbitration and when an officer or employee of the agency has authority to settle an issue in controversy through binding arbitration.
